info.title: Cetec ERP API Documentation
info.description:
This documentation shows you how to access the actual data of the current site that you are logged into. If you choose to make any PUT, POST, or DELETE calls, you will be changing live data and these actions are not reversible! At this time there is no versioning guarantees in regards to parameters & responses. Additive changes may be provided without communication; Breaking changes will be communicated to users in the release notes

All Support/Assistance For API Work And Related Issues Will Be Billed!

tags.name: Accounting
tags.description: AP, AR, UnappliedCash, Credit Memos, etc

tags.name: Config
tags.description: Includes setup / core data. Address in the UI, by going to Admin > Config Settings.

tags.name: Contacts
tags.description: The Contact API provides methods to get individual Contacts, collections of Contacts, create new Contacts, and modify Contacts.

tags.name: Customer Address
tags.description: Setup and retrieve Customer Addresses (Shipto, Billto, etc)

tags.name: DataMaintenance
tags.description: Includes setup / core data. ECO Change Types to FOBCodes.

tags.name: Part Spec Group
tags.description: For Part replacement on a BOM or an Order Temp BOM

tags.name: Parts
tags.description: The Part API provides methods to interact with individual parts and collections of parts.

tags.name: Cross Parts
tags.description: The Cross Parts API provides methods to retrieve and manage cross part and synonym information associated with parts.

tags.name: PartRevision Labor Plan
tags.description: Access to Part Revision Labor Plan

tags.name: Purchasing
tags.description: Purchase Orders, and other purchasing specific calls

tags.name: PO Line Receipts
tags.description: PO Line Receipts tie to PO Lines (and POs) as a record of receipt for incoming material

tags.name: Prcs
tags.description: Product Codes are the first 3 chars of a Cetec PRCPart (unique part identifier)

tags.name: Quotes
tags.description: The Quote API provides methods to interact with individual quotes and collections of quotes.

tags.name: RMA
tags.description: API for RMA Creation and lookup

tags.name: Tags
tags.description: Reference Tags or QuoteLine Tags by API

tags.name: User
tags.description: User APIs including get user info and list

get.description: This API provides access to Bonus Columns for objects in the system.  A valid user session or the preshared token is required.

parameters.name: object_type
parameters.description: The ClassName of the object to retrieve bonus columns for: 'Part', 'Customer', 'Contact', 'Quote', 'QuoteLine', 'Project', 'PQuote', 'PQuoteLine','FIFOIX', 'VendorReturn', 'SkidHead', 'SkidLine', 'Tool', 'Vendor', 'Ordhead', 'Invoice', 'Forecast','NCR', 'CustomerComplaint', 'RMAHeader', 'CommissionDefault','CommissionBreakdown', 'PartRevisionDefinition'

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

200.description: returns the bonus columns for that object

x-codeSamples.source:
curl --request GET \
  --url 'http://undefinedundefined/api/bonuscolumn?object_type=SOME_STRING_VALUE&preshared_token=SOME_STRING_VALUE' \
  --header 'content-type: multipart/form-data' \
  --data null

package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"strings"
	"net/http"
	"io/ioutil"
)

func main() {

	url := "http://undefinedundefined/api/bonuscolumn?object_type=SOME_STRING_VALUE&preshared_token=SOME_STRING_VALUE"

	payload := strings.NewReader("null")

	req, _ := http.NewRequest("GET", url, payload)

	req.Header.Add("content-type", "multipart/form-data")

	res, _ := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)

	defer res.Body.Close()
	body, _ := ioutil.ReadAll(res.Body)

	fmt.Println(res)
	fmt.Println(string(body))

}

import http.client

conn = http.client.HTTPConnection("undefinedundefined")

payload = "null"

headers = { 'content-type': "multipart/form-data" }

conn.request("GET", "/api/bonuscolumn?object_type=SOME_STRING_VALUE&preshared_token=SOME_STRING_VALUE", payload, headers)

res = conn.getresponse()
data = res.read()

print(data.decode("utf-8"))

put.description: This API provides access to create a Customer Address in the system, as well as a link to it in the HTTP Location header. Must have valid Customer by id to complete this call.

requestBody.description: Customer Address Put Payload

country_id.description: country_ids are retrievable using the address_country api.

tax_group_id.description: If this address is taxable, and that tax group is setup, you can search for it using this field. First, Cetec searches for a 9TAX by id, if none found, searches by tax_description.

200.description: successful operation -- will return the following object

put.description:
This API provides access to create a Customer in the system, as well as a link to it in the HTTP Location header.
If a customer already exists in the system with the same name, you must use force_create: 1 in the body of the request.
After creating a customer, you can use the returned `id` to add an address using the `PUT /api/customeraddress` endpoint.

requestBody.description: Customer Put Payload

name.description: the name to create.  If an existing customer is found with this name, that Customer will be returned.

200.description: successful operation -- will return the following object

getCustomerById.description: A link to retrieve the customer that was just created. The `id` from the response body is used as the `id` parameter for the `getCustomer` operation.

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

parameters.description: External Key of Customer

parameters.name: to_date
parameters.description: the Invoice shipdate YYYY:MM:DD HH:mm:SS

parameters.name: invoicedate_from
parameters.description: From Invoice date YYYY:MM:DD HH:mm:SS

parameters.name: invoicedate_to
parameters.description: To Invoice date YYYY:MM:DD HH:mm:SS

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Default rows count for this API is 5000. Using page=2 will give you records 5001-10000. If rows is specified, will give you records: (1 + (page * rows)) -> (rows + (page * rows)).

parameters.name: rows
parameters.description: Default rows count for this API is 5000. Use this parameter to specify something different. Setting this higher than 5000 will result in moderate latency for the response of this API.

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description: Order by directly interfaces with the database. For this reason, you must use the table alias (me) & column names provided in the response. Suggestions include: 'me.invoicedate asc', 'me.id desc', 'me.total_invoice_amount desc', 'customer.ytd_sales desc'. Attempting to sort by text fields is available, but discouraged. Depending on the contents of the columns may yield unexpected results.

parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

200.description: successful operation -- will return an array of the following object

get.summary: Gets a single Order Object by id, or Ordernum (without location code)
get.description:
(the Order ID should be provided after the final slash. An Ordernum [e.g. 2.1] may also be used [detected due to the period in the order number.])
custponum is the Customer PO Number
billto_* is the Bill To Address Information
shipto_* is the Ship To Address Information
quote_id is the Quote ID that created this Order deleted_flag In the header context, 1 if the Order has been closed. Note that the flag will also exist at the line level, and will indicate if the line is open or closed.
tracking_numbers is an array of tracking_numbers for this order.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of Object

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

requestBody.description: create_invoice: true will create invoice if lines are Charge / NN. fails if any lines in order are not charge

create_invoice.description: Creates an Invoice if lines are Charge / NN. Will fail if there are non-charge lines

ordlines.description: An array of order lines to be updated.

hold_comments.description: Comments that you want listed on the hold.

placed_by.description: Include a username, or string that you wanted listed as the user on this hold.

add_tracking_numbers.description: Include a tracking number or comma delineated tracking numbers to add to the order

200.description: Returns a single Order with lines & Customer

patch.description: Modify tags, valid_to/from, public, protected, active, or move objects. A valid user session or the preshared token in body is required.

requestBody.description: Don't include keys that you don't want to be updated.

active.description: if set to 1, active. If 2, pending, if 0, deleted.

object_id.description: id of object document is related to (integer). For Parts, use the full PRCPart (string).

object_type.description: The ClassName of the object to retrieve notes for: CAR, CheckRegister, Customer, CustomerComplaint, Document, ECO, GLEntry, Invoice, NCR, Ordhead, Part, PO, POLineReceipt, PQuote, Process, Quote, RMAHeader, Tool, UnappliedCash, Vendor, VendorReturn, Voucher

tags.description: comma separated list of tags that you want the document to have.

valid_from.description: If set, document only valid after date.

valid_to.description: If set, document only valid before date.

public.description: Determines wether document is publically viewable.

protected.description: If set to 1, then only Admins, Users with the IT role, and anyone with the API Token will be able to see or download the document

make_global.description: If set, document will become global

unmake_global.description: If set, document will no longer be global (requires valid object_type & object_id).

200.description: successful operation -- will return an array of the following object

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

post.summary: Get Multiple Docs (New)
post.description: Get a batch of documents and sort/filter. A valid user session or the preshared token in body is required.

requestBody.description: Enables easy pagination.

active.description: if set to 1, active. If 2, pending, if 0, deleted.

object_id.description: id of object document is related to (integer). For Parts, use the full PRCPart (string).

object_type.description: The ClassName of the object to retrieve notes for: CAR, CheckRegister, Customer, CustomerComplaint, Document, ECO, GLEntry, Invoice, NCR, Ordhead, Part, PO, POLineReceipt, PQuote, Process, Quote, RMAHeader, Tool, UnappliedCash, Vendor, VendorReturn, Voucher

tags.description: comma separated list of tags that you want the document to have.

all_revs.description: If set, call will return all revisions of the documents.

received_count.description: Determines offset in query for pagination.

order_by.description: Defaults to id. Valid choices include: id name object_type object_id revision active url_to_doc filename filesize valid_from valid_to user_id public protected is_object_image created_at is_imported imported_date

order_desc.description: If set to true, documents will be sorted in descending order, if false ascending.

200.description: successful operation -- will return an array of the following object

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

file.description: The path you want to upload (eg: "file=@./example.png")

object_id.description: id of object document is related to (integer). For Parts, use the full PRCPart (string).

public.description: If set to 1, able to download without logging in. (used for logo)

protected.description: If set to 1, then only Admins, Users with the IT role, and anyone with the API Token will be able to see or download the document

tags.description: comma separated list of tags that you want the returned documents to all have.

active.description: either 1, or 2. if set to 1 will be set as active. If 2, will be set as pending. Must be updated with PATCH later.

put.description: Create a work segment via API.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Ordline ID

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

requestBody.description: OrdlineWork Create (Start Work) Payload

active.description: If set to 0, then it will create a completed OrdlineWork resource. If stop isn't specified, it will default to the current time. The start param must be specified.

comments.description: short text of what was completed during this work segment.

force_overtime.description: Forces calculation with overtime labor rates. 0|1

pieces_completed.description: number pieces completed during this work segment.

start.description: If not included, uses, 'now'. Stop must be after Start. Format is 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:SS'

stop.description: If included, it will create a completed OrdlineWork resource. The start param must be specified. Stop must be after Start. Format is 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:SS'

user_id.description: - If set, then it will create a completed OrdlineWork resource. The start param must be specified.

201.description: Returns the ordline work record you just created.

post.summary: Single Bin Update or Create.
post.description:
(Update or create) bin.

Warning! This is very dangerous and can have unrecoverable inventory consequences. Please use with caution. You don't need a bin_id if you have bin, prcpart, and location. Consider the following scenario (starting with no bins or qoh for this part):

{
  "bin": "ANEWBIN",
  "prcpart": "PRT1",
  "message_text": "Updated by XRay Counter",
  "adjust_qty": 5,
  "location": "MN",
  "cost": 32.03
}

Creates a new bin with qty 5.

{
  "bin": "ANEWBIN",
  "prcpart": "PRT1",
  "message_text": "Updated by XRay Counter",
  "adjust_qty": 2,
  "location": "MN"
}

Adds to the bin from previous step (ending qty 7)

{
  "bin": "ANEWBIN",
  "prcpart": "PRT1",
  "message_text": "Updated by XRay Counter",
  "adjust_qty": -3,
  "location": "MN"
}

Subtracts to the bin from previous step (ending qty 4)

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

requestBody.description: Bin POST Payload

bin_id.description: If Bin id is known and provided, will attempt to update.

location.description: 2 char location code where bin is, eg: MN

message_text.description: What's happening to the bin & why. (Receiving, converting, etc.), if not provided, assumes 'Scrap'; Correlates to an InvShortageReason name from Admin > Maintenance > Data Maintenance > InvShortageReason

cost.description: bin unit cost - only include if an adjustment is appropriate, or if this call will result in a new bin being created.

who_last_maintained.description: username of the last person to update the bin

moveToBin.description: After receiving a part, use this link to move it to a new bin location. The `id` and `cost` from this response are used to populate the `fifoix_id` and `cost` in the request body for the move operation.

post.summary: Single Bin Move
post.description:
Move the contents of a bin. May Scrap material. If moving results in a negative balance, bins will be adjusted back to 0.
Warning! This is very dangerous and can have unrecoverable inventory consequences. Please use with caution.

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

requestBody.description: Receiving Move Payload

location.description: 2 char location code where bin is, eg: MN

quantity_to_move.description: Quantity to move from bin to bin_to. If quantity_to_move > qty in bin, then will increase the quantity in bin, so it will result in 0, then transfer the quantity_to_move specified in the call to bin_to. (Will have ledger implications if quantity_to_move > Qoh

scrap.description: If Scrap, adds stock to inventory with Inv Shortage Reason of 'Scrap' in the bin, then relieves it from that bin. (Ledger implications on both sides).

adjustQuantity.description: After moving a part, use this link to make a quantity adjustment. The `fifoix_id` from the move response is used as the `id` path parameter for the adjustment operation.

parameters.name: include_can_build

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description: Order by directly interfaces with the database. For this reason, you must use the column names provided in the response. Suggestions include: 'created_on asc', 'last_sold desc', 'weight desc'. Attempting to sort by text fields is available, but discouraged. Depending on the contents of the columns may yield unexpected results.

parameters.name: rows

patch.description: Updates a part in the database via Body Param rather than URL Param. This is the preferred method for updating a part; and is required if the part has any characters other than alphanumeric.

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

requestBody.description: Part PATCH Payload

prcpart.description: The full PRCPart number/name to be updated

cos_gl_account_id.description: Choosing an Account will drive how the COS for this Prcpart is accounted for when invoiced.

default_receiving_location.description: The default receiving location, if different from the system default

default_selling_location.description: Provide the default selling location, if different from the system default

inventory_gl_account_id.description: Choosing an Account will drive how the Inventory for this Prcpart.

last_resort_cost.description: Used to set the Part's default cost if no pricing rules are found

measure_uom_id.description: ID from the UOM table for WidthxHeightxDepth measurements

preferred_vendor_name.description: If defined and blank in the query, and preferred_vendor_id is defined in query, we will clear the preferred vendor. WARNING!!!

revenue_gl_account_id.description: Account ID, not the account number or name

shipping_instructions.description: Instructions to use when shipping this part

uom_id.description: The ID of the UOM the system use to consume inventory of this part, from UOM data table; WARNING!!! Changing this has major implications on products already shipped as well as current inventory.

warranty_unit.description: Valid entries are days, months, or years

default_suborder.description: Default Suborder. 0 (follow global default), 1 (Skip Build), 2 (With Top), 3 (Always Build)

put.summary: Single PUT (Upload) a Part. Creates a new part, or updates if already exists.
put.description: Create a single part in the database

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

requestBody.description: Part Put Payload

location.description: Provide a location to create an inventory record. Must use location codes already defined in the system. Must be an array, even if only one location is provided.

inventory_gl_account_id.description: Choosing an Account will drive how the Inventory for this Prcpart.

cos_gl_account_id.description: Choosing an Account will drive how the COS for this Prcpart is accounted for when invoiced.

create_fixbom.description: Creates the part as a BOM if true/1

created_on.description: The part's created on date, YYYY-MM-DD

default_receiving_location.description: The default receiving location, if different from the system default

default_selling_location.description: Provide the default selling location, if different from the system default

last_resort_cost.description: Used to set the Part's default cost if no pricing rules are found

measure_uom_id.description: ID from the UOM table for WidthxHeightxDepth measurements

preferred_vendor_name.description: If defined and blank in the query, and preferred_vendor_id is defined in query, we will clear the preferred vendor. WARNING!!!

primary_bin.description: Provide the primary bin name/number (system will default to NEW)

purchase_uom_id.description: From the UOM table, by id

revenue_gl_account_id.description: Account ID, not the account number or name

shipping_instructions.description: Instructions to use when shipping this part

uom_id.description: The ID of the UOM the system use to consume inventory of this part, from UOM data table; WARNING!!! Changing this has major implications on products already shipped as well as current inventory.

warranty_unit.description: Valid entries are days, months, or years

default_suborder.description: Default Suborder. 0 (follow global default), 1 (Skip Build), 2 (With Top), 3 (Always Build)

parameters.name: prcpart

parameters.name: filters[0][name]
parameters.description: filters default filter by most columns. Must include all 3 for each index: name, value, search-type

parameters.name: filters[0][value]
parameters.description: filters default filter by most columns. Must include all 3 for each index: name, value, search-type

parameters.name: filters[0][search-type]
parameters.description: filters default filter by most columns. Must include all 3 for each index: name, value, search-type. search-type valid values are: eq,eq-mul,ne,right_like,left_like,full_like,gt,lt,lte,gte

post.summary: Creates or updates a BOM (PartRevisionDefinitions) with payload
post.description: Pass the header Version: 2.0. This endpoint can create or update BOM structures including sub-assemblies.

parameters.name: Version
parameters.description: 2.0 is the only valid value. Don't include otherwise

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

requestBody.description: For the external Keys, you need to set these values up in Data Maintenance (in their respective tables) prior to them working properly.  You can submit multiple layers of BOMs by including the bom_definition object within a bom_definition object.

comp_num.description: Numeric; Gets overridden by 'component' if component is provided. Used for lookup to see if component already exists (comp_num / component, component_prcpart, revision_id). If all 3 are provided, and there is a match, the associated record will be updated, instead of a new one created.

component.description: Numeric; Overrides comp_num if both provided. Used for lookup to see if component already exists (comp_num / component, component_prcpart, revision_id). If all 3 are provided, and there is a match, the associated record will be updated, instead of a new one created.

component_prcpart.description: Child part number (prcpart) of the component

revision_id.description: Specific revision ID of the component part

reference_designator.description: Comma-separated designators for PCB/electronic assemblies (e.g., R1,R2,C3)

deleted_flag.description: Mark component as deleted instead of removing from payload

instructions.description: Array of string instructions to include

operation_external_key.description: Be sure to set the external Key in Data Maintenance

setup.description: If Setup, only charges once per any qty

pick_comments.example: pick_comments Pick Length if you want it

sourcing.example: You can have grandkids as well. Be sure not to create Recursive BOMs

pick_comments.example: pick_comments Pick Length if you want it

200.description: Returns the created/updated part revision definition with complete BOM structure

x-codeSamples.source:
curl --request POST \
  --url 'http://undefinedundefined/api/partrevisiondefinition?preshared_token=SOME_STRING_VALUE' \
  --header 'Version: SOME_STRING_VALUE' \

  --data '{"bom_definition":[{"comp_num":1,"component":1,"component_prcpart":"CHILD1","revision_id":42,"reference_designator":"R1,R2,R3","deleted_flag":false,"locations":[{"build_order":0,"operations":[{"instructions":["string"],"operation_external_key":"string","place_in_line":0,"repetitions":0,"setup":true}]}],"pick_comments":"pick_comments Pick Length if you want it","ordline_status_id":"Use the OrdlineStatus (Work Location) table","prcpart":"SUB2","qty_per_top":1.2,"scrap_adder":1,"scrap_factor":"5%","revision_text":"A","sourcing":"You can have grandkids as well. put.summary: Returns the PQuote with the ID supplied

requestBody.description: Directly edit a BOM or component record by id.

carrier_acct_num.description: The carrier account number on the PQuote.

cost_center_id.description: The Cost Center for the PQuote

fob_code.description: The FOB to put on the PQuote.  The default will be used if not provided.

location.description: The warehouse location where the PQuote will be recieved. Will default to the default selling location if not given.

owner_id.description: The user who will be the owner of the PQuote

po_comment.description: Sets the comment that will transfer to the PO when the PQuote is converted.

pquote_comment.description: An internal comment on the PQuote

scheduled.description: Sets whether the PQuote is scheduled.  Will check the config 'pquote_default_scheduled' if not provided.

ship_via_code.description: The 'Code' of the ship via.  The default ship via will be used if one is not provided

ship_via_external_key.description: Unique key for a 'SHPCDE'.  The default ship via from the config setting 'pquote_default_ship_via' will be used if one is not  provided

ship_via_id.description: ID of the 'SHPCDE' used for the PQuote.  The default ship via will be used if one is not provided

vendor_external_key.description: Unique Key to find a vendor to add to the PQuote.  Required if not providing a name or vendor id.

vendor_id.description: The ID for the Vendor to be added on the PQuote.  Required if not passing a providing a vendor to create or vendor external key.

vendor_name.description: Can be used to find a vendor to be added to the PQuote.  It is suggested to use ID or external key over name.  If the vendor doesn't exist it will be created from this name.

200.description: successful operation -- will return the PQuote for the given ID

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

post.summary: Import Quote (Create Order)

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

requestBody.description:
It is possible to import data for bonus columns that you've set up. This can be done by referencing the 'Technical Name' of the bonus column, which can be found by going to /bonuscolumns/Quote/edit (for header Bonus Columns) or /bonuscolumns/QuoteLine/edit (for line level Bonus Columns). The value you enter will depend on the data type of your bonus column.

A prepayment can be created for the quote by populating the payment_type_id with the system id for one of the valid payment types. Additionally you will need to populate the prepayment_amount and the prepayment_ref. The value of the prepayment can be set to the total quote amount by passing the string 'FULL' in the prepayment_amount. When using 'FULL', you can set freight_billing_type to 'fixed_bid' to prevent calculated freight costs from affecting the quote total.

If tax is externally collected/tracked, pass the tax amount to tax_collected and set taxtype to 2.

customer_custnum.description: If customer_id not provided, can do lookup using customer_custnum

customer_id.description: the Cetec-ERP ID of the Quote's Customer.

customer_name.description: If customer_id not provided, and customer_custnum doesn't match, can do lookup using customer_name. (Creates a customer if no match)

external_key.example: My external system unique identifier - must be unique, or do not provide

internal_customer_id.description:
Internal Customer ID is required for doing internal build orders (Lines with transcode: SA, that you want to put on shelf instead of ship directly to customer). Commonly, the ID is 1, though your environment may include additional internal customers. 
Additionally, if creating a linked workorder this is used if the customer record you provided with the customer name was found to not be intercompany, we'll use this to try to look up an internal customer.

internal_vendor_id.description: Internal Vendor ID is required for doing internal build orders (Lines with transcode: SA, that you want to put on shelf instead of ship directly to customer). Commonly, the ID is 1, though your environment may include additional internal vendors.

payment_type_id.description: System ID for a valid payment type, populate this if you want to record a prepayment for the quote.

prepayment_amount.description: Either the amount of the prepayment without a currency symbol or the string 'FULL' in order to apply the full value of the quote.

prepayment_ref.description: The reference string for the prepayment.

cost.description: Unless force_overwrite_cost_breaks is true, this value will be overwritten from the system data (default cost, etc). Also, cost is recalculated at time of Invoice (All Costs are Estimates until Invoice).

external_key.description: Not required. Used for looking this line up for future edits. If provided, must be unique across all order lines.
external_key.example: Not required. If provided, must be unique across all order lines.

force_overwrite_cost_breaks.description: If force_overwrite_cost_breaks is true, uses cost provided. Else, recalculates cost from rules

force_resale.description: If force_resale is true, uses resale key provided. Else, recalculates resale from rules.

locations.description: place_order must be true in order for the locations to be saved, as this field is for Order Line Level Labor Plan. If you want to create Part Revision Labor plans, use that API instead.

instructions.description: Array of string instructions to include

operation_external_key.description: Be sure to set the external Key in Data Maintenance on the BuildOperation table

setup.description: If Setup, only charges 1ce per any qty

qty.description: The quantity of the part being built/sold.

resale.description: Unless force_resale is true, this value will be overwritten from the system data (default resale & pricing rules)

transcode.description: Transcode must be provided. Valid options are SN, SA, NN. SN = Stock, SA = Build, NN = Charge. Do not include anything other than the 2 characters for this field.

use_current_rev.description: Set true to get current revision for this prcpart, otherwise can specify revision with revision key

revision.description: revision text. Use this to specify a specific revision instead of current revision. Ignored if use_current_rev is true.

wip_date.description: Work Start Date in YYYY-MM-DD format

link_workorder_ordernum.description: If provided, will link created order to this workorder_ordernum. Will attempt to link to whichever order is provided here.

place_order.description: Set to true to create the order from this quote. Otherwise, don't include.

sales_import_source_id.description: Used to determine a quotes source (manual, webservice1,2, app, etc)

ship_via.description: First looks up CustomerShipviaCode by external_key, then from SHPCDE table by external_key, then SHPCDE table by code, then SHPCDE table by 'method_of_shipment' LIKE 'value%'. Best results if you use external_key, or code. If none provided, will select the default

post.description: Update a bin (change the expires, mfg_lot_code, country_origin, date_code, is_locked, or bonus columns).
post.summary: Updates the contents of a bin

parameters.description: id of the bin
parameters.name: id
preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

country_origin.description: country of origin for the bin. This is used for export compliance. As of 2025-05-21 in Cetec it is a freeform field. However, it is recommended to use the ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code for the country of origin. For example, US for United States, CA for Canada, etc. This is a freeform field and can be set to any value. It is not validated against a list of countries as of 2025-05-21. *subject to change*

date_code.description: exact match to date code of bin

mfg_lot_code.description: exact match to lot code of bin

allocate_to_ordline_id.description: Caution! This will allocate the bin to the order line by order line id. It will have waterfall, purchasing/MRP consequences.

allocate_to_customer_id.description: Caution! This will allocate the bin to the customer by customer id. It will have waterfall, purchasing/MRP consequences.

is_locked.description: true if you want locked bins, false if only unlocked

who_last_maintained.description: username that last maintained. If none provided, will use techx (system user).

bonus_bc.description: honor the type of your bonus column

403.description: error: This endpoint only supported with preshared_token at this time

get.description: Active Order & Part need | Running this request in the browser will crash your browser. The purpose of inclusion is for documentation purposes only.

parameters.description: key of which data to retrieve. Valid values are: allocation, demand, inventory, supply, vendor_parts, full
parameters.name: key
prcparts
parameters.description: Optional list of prcparts to filter results. Applies to 'supply' and 'demand'. Pass multiple values like: ?prcparts=PRT1&prcparts=PRT2

parameters.name: component_prcparts
parameters.description: Optional list of prcparts to filter results by component.  Applies to 'demand'. Differs from the prcparts filter because this one looks for demand by component, rather than by BOM on Build Orders. Still filters by this field on Stock orders. Pass multiple values like: ?component_prcparts=PRT1&component_prcparts=PRT2

If included and true, is used in the demand response. Changes the way Part Spec Rules evaluate to be exact match to their prcpart, instead of a wildcard match. This drastically improves performance, but is only valid in datasets where each part spec rule exactly matches its assocated part (by prcpart).
parameters.name: exact_psg
rows
parameters.description: Optional. Applies to 'supply' and 'demand' (and the supply and demand sections of the 'full' key). Number of rows per page (default 20)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Optional. Applies to 'supply' and 'demand' (and the supply and demand sections of the 'full' key). Page number for pagination (default 1)

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

200.description: with the keys from the key field, and applicable data within each key

403.description: error: This endpoint only supported with preshared_token at this time"

  --header 'content-type: multipart/form-data' \
  --data null

package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"strings"
	"net/http"
	"io/ioutil"
)

func main() {

	url := "http://undefinedundefined/goapis/api/v1/customer/lite_list?preshared_token=SOME_STRING_VALUE"

	payload := strings.NewReader("null")

	req, _ := http.NewRequest("GET", url, payload)

	req.Header.Add("content-type", "multipart/form-data")

	res, _ := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)

	defer res.Body.Close()
	body, _ := ioutil.ReadAll(res.Body)

	fmt.Println(res)
	fmt.Println(string(body))

}

import http.client

conn = http.client.HTTPConnection("undefinedundefined")

payload = "null"

headers = { 'content-type': "multipart/form-data" }

conn.request("GET", "/goapis/api/v1/customer/lite_list?preshared_token=SOME_STRING_VALUE", payload, headers)

res = conn.getresponse()
data = res.read()

print(data.decode("utf-8"))

deleted_by_id.description: deleted_by_id -- only include if deleted response

deleted_on.description: deleted_on -- only include if deleted response

setup.description: setup (bool). determines whether to multiply the work time by the qty

part_revision_map_id.description: part_revision_map_id -- used for linking to partrevisionmap.

recurring_time.description: recurring_time is a calculated field. Do not include it in API POST/PATCH

setup_time.description: setup_time is a calculated field. Do not include it in API POST/PATCH

requestBody.description: json formatted string param. using id of objects is preferred, external_key & in some cases name are available for when you don't know the id. build_order & place_in_line are both required fields.

403.description: errors: couldn't create operation

patch.description:
Updates specified fields for an ordline (workorder line).

To update the location, first retrieve the [ordline_maps](/apioverview/react#tag/Order-Lines/paths/~1goapis~1api~1v1~1ordline~1%7Bordline_id%7D~1location_maps/get) using the [`GET /goapis/api/v1/ordline/{ordline_id}/location_maps`](/apioverview/react#tag/Order-Lines/paths/~1goapis~1api~1v1~1ordline~1%7Bordline_id%7D~1location_maps/get) endpoint.

This endpoint returns a list of ordline work locations . You can then use either:
- `location_id`: ID of the target location (ordline status)
- `id`: ID of the work location (ordline_map_id)

🟡 Only the fields included in the request payload will be updated. If you want to update only the location, include only the `location` or `ordline_map_id` in the request body.
patch.summary: Update a single ordline by ID

parameters.description: ID of the ordline to update
parameters.name: ordline_id
preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

move_to_next.description: move the order line to the next location

requestBody.description: The following fields are available to update using this endpoint. Only pass in the fields you want to update, passing in empty values will overwrite any already set values. Bonus columns can also be updated using the technical name of the column in the request body.

  --data '{"custpart":"string","line_comment":"string","ncnr":true,"priority_rank":0,"r_and_d":true,"schedule_locked":true,"sourcing_comments":"string","tech_comments":"string","location":0,"ordline_map_id":0,"move_to_next":true}'

patch.summary: Move work order to new location
patch.description:
Updates the location status of a specific order line (ordline) and sends notifications.

To update the location, first retrieve the [ordline_maps](/apioverview/react#tag/Order-Lines/paths/~1goapis~1api~1v1~1ordline~1%7Bordline_id%7D~1location_maps/get) using the [`GET /goapis/api/v1/ordline/{ordline_id}/location_maps`](/apioverview/react#tag/Order-Lines/paths/~1goapis~1api~1v1~1ordline~1%7Bordline_id%7D~1location_maps/get) endpoint.

This endpoint returns a list of ordline work locations . You can then use either:
- `location_id`: ID of the target location (ordline status)
- `id`: ID of the work location (ordline_map_id)

The ordline_status can be set independently if desired.

parameters.description: id of the user
parameters.name: user_id
id
parameters.description: ID of the ordline to update

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

requestBody.description: Update ordline location and status. Only include keys that you want to update; ordline location can be set alone.

complete_schedule.description: Flag to mark schedule as complete

ordline_status.description: Status of the ordline (optional, can be set alone)

use_location_id.description: Flag to indicate  location id  assigned, it's required in case you are not setting the ordline_map_id

move_to_next.description: move the order line to the next location

200.description: successful operation -- ordline moved to new location

400.description: bad request -- invalid input or ordline doesn't exist

  --data '{"ordline_map_id":456,"complete_schedule":false,"ordline_status":{"id":1,"description":"In Progress"},"location_id":789,"use_location_id":false,"move_to_next":false,"user_id":false}'

post.description: Returns Pick List for Orders. Response depends on what's provided. If a single order_term is provided, this returns the components []BomOrder & their bins []Fifoix.\nIf multiple orders are provided, just returns array of { prcpart: string, ext_qty_required : float, qty_picked: float}
post.summary: Returns Pick List for Orders

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

bomorder_id.description: data:"33   use this in tandem with the /ordlines/pick_list response to only include data about those components. By id, not by compnum"`

component_linenums.description: use this in tandem with the /ordlines/pick_list response to only include data about those components. By component_linenum, not by id"`

order_term.description: array of search terms in form MN27.1-3 Do not include leading 0's

prcpart.description: array of TLA prcparts. Currently, only 1st is honored, this may change in the future. We'll search for orders that use this prcpart within the date_from / date_to

transcode.description: valid options: SA,SN,NN. Only honored if order_term is empty & prcpart has 1 or more records. SA (build), SN (stock), NN (charge). If not provided, or doesn't match, SA (build) is default.

requestBody.description: Must be in form MN27.1-3. If any orders are build, only build order information will be returned. (we'll skip the stock orders if there are any mixed in). If only stock orders, we'll return sum of shipqty if > 0, or sum balance due.

403.description: error: error

post.summary: Create and Update Resale Price Breaks
post.description:
Create new resale price breaks and update existing ones for a given prcpart.

Requires a valid prcpart query parameter.
Expects a JSON array of resale price break objects.
Existing breaks are updated, and new ones are inserted.

parameters.name: prcpart
parameters.description: prcpart to add/update resale price breaks for

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

requestBody.description: Array of resale price breaks

id.description: Required only when updating an existing break

from_qty.description: Required when creating a new break

to_qty.description: Required when creating a new break

price.description: Required when creating a new break

400.description: Bad request (e.g., missing prcpart, invalid payload, or prcpart does not exist)

post.summary: Create and Update Cost Price Breaks
post.description:
Create new cost price breaks and update existing ones for a given prcpart.

Requires a valid prcpart query parameter.
Expects a JSON array of cost price break objects.
Existing breaks are updated, and new ones are inserted.

- If `vendor_id = 0`, it is treated as a simple cost price break.
- If `vendor_id > 0`, it is treated as a vendor-specific cost price break.
- Response differs depending on whether new breaks were created or only updates were applied.

parameters.name: prcpart
parameters.description: prcpart to add/update cost price breaks for

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

requestBody.description: Array of cost price breaks

id.description: Required only when updating an existing break

from_qty.description: Required when creating a new break

to_qty.description: Required when creating a new break

price.description: Required when creating a new break

vendor_id.description: If 0 = general cost break, >0 = vendor-specific break

400.description: Bad request (e.g., missing prcpart, invalid payload, or prcpart does not exist)

post.summary: Update And Create Cross Parts
post.description:
Create, update, or delete cross parts for a given part ID.

- If `id` is missing in the request body, a new cross part is inserted.
- If `is_drop = true`, the cross part is deleted.
- If `id` is present and `is_drop = false`, the cross part is updated.

This API also handles updating default flags and bonus columns for cross parts.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the base part for which cross part information is updated

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

id.description: ID of the cross part (null for insert)

association.description: Associated Customer, Vendor, or Manufacturer name

cross_type_id.description: Type ID of the cross part (e.g., Customer Part, Vendor Part, Manufacturer Part)

notes.description: Additional notes for the cross part

default_for_type.description: Whether this cross part is the default for its type

is_drop.description: If true, this cross part will be deleted

bonus_columns.description: Dynamic bonus column data for the cross part

400.description: Invalid request (bad payload or invalid part ID)

500.description: Server error while updating cross part information

patch.description: Updates a purchase order line using either PO Line ID (id) or PONum (ponum)/LineNum(linenum) combination. Requires preshared token.

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

requestBody.description: PO Line update data

id.description: PO Line ID. Only required if no ponum & linenum available.

ponum.description: PO Number. Required with linenum if no PO LINE ID not available.

linenum.description: PO Line Number. Required with PO Number if no PO Line ID available.

400.description: Bad request - invalid data or missing required fields

404.description: PO or PO Line not found

post.description: Specify the capacities you want the finite scheduling engine to use for each location from this date.
post.summary: Copy from the date range to the date range

parameters.name: preshared_token
parameters.description: Preshared API Token from Admin > Config > Config Settings

from.description: YYYY-MM-DD date to copy from -- can be a range with 2 dates. We'll copy all the days from between and including those dates while respecing the day of week. HOWEVER, if it's a single date, we'll copy that date's capacity to the full range in the 'to' parameter -- day of week limitations don't apply if 1 date is used for 'From'

to.description: YYYY-MM-DD date / range to copy to. can be a range with 2 dates. second provided must be after first. Please note that larger ranges will take some time.

requestBody.description: body must contain the following properties

200.description: success: this call for the date range was successful

403.description: error: This endpoint only supported with preshared_token at this time"
